summary: Round 19 preview is live with seven tips: Warriors, Sharks, Bulldogs, Eels, Knights, Sea Eagles and Storm. No Round 19 charity bet is locked, so the Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains 3-2 and +$0.53.

sources.label: NRL Late Mail Round 19: All eyes on Origin stars; Dearden nears return
2026 NRL season results: Round 19 fixture table

scoreboard.note: Warriors lose Barnett and Capewell from the extended squad but regain James Fisher-Harris and Leka Halasima. Tigers regain Luai but lose Api Koroisau to a pectoral injury.
Sharks keep Hynes and Trindall together. Dolphins are set to lose Jack Bostock after an Origin head knock and still need final calls on several Origin reserves.
Home lean only. Crichton is expected to back up for Canterbury; Raiders are boosted by Papalii and Noah Martin but still carry Origin back-up uncertainty around Young and Strange.
Upset lean. Roosters are set to lose James Tedesco after an Origin head knock, while Mitchell Moses is expected to back up for Parramatta.
Ponga and Bradman Best are expected to back up, and Dylan Brown remains the key spine edge. Souths regain Cameron Murray and Campbell Graham but lose Cody Walker to suspension.
Manly home lean with Tom Trbojevic, Jamal Fogarty, Tolu Koula and Haumole Olakau'atu named. Cowboys have Cotter, Nanai and Taulagi set to back up, with Tom Dearden nearing a return.
Top confidence tip. Melbourne regain Hughes, Meaney and Howarth, while Munster, Grant and Loiero are named to back up from Origin.

liveAlerts.text: Round 19 preview is live: tips are Warriors, Sharks, Bulldogs, Eels, Knights, Sea Eagles and Storm.
Charity ledger remains 3-2, $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
No Round 19 charity bet is locked; Storm and Sharks are price-watch candidates only.
Late mail matters: Tedesco and Bostock are set to miss, and several Origin players are still monitored.
